summary.intent: Address Codex review comments on PR #694 in ars-ui, specifically fixing controlled rating reverts, TagGroup activation key handling, and half-step behavior in rating components.
summary.outcome: Two commits were pushed (f39ae9b2 and a follow-up): first covering already-identified issues, second patching controlled revert cases, tag activation keys, and public-props half mode. Full coverage passed at 90.1% branch coverage for ars-components.

repo: ars-ui uses cargo xtask for CI tasks including coverage thresholds (cargo xtask ci coverage) and spec validation (cargo xtask spec validate).
Snapshot testing uses cargo-insta: tests are run per-crate with `cargo insta test -p ars-components --lib -- <module>`.
Coverage gate is branch-based with a threshold; ars-components must maintain ≥90% branch coverage.
Codex posts review threads on GitHub PRs via GraphQL API; threads are queried with gh api graphql to enumerate unresolved comments.

code.finding: Rating group logic spans lines 30-155 (props/state) and 835-930 (event handling); controlled mode revert behavior and half-step logic (Props { allow_half: true, step: 1.0 }) required fixes.

TagGroup selection activation keys (Enter/Space via KeyboardKey enum) were missing; on_tag_keydown handler needed updating. Controlled toggle revert logic also patched. Key handling spans ~lines 1708-1798.

workflow: Codex review threads arrive asynchronously; a second batch appeared while CI coverage was running, requiring a second patch commit rather than amending the first.
Workflow order: fmt → unit tests → clippy → spec validate → insta snapshots → full coverage before pushing.
Co-authorship attribution to Codex is added via git commit trailer (Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>).

friction: Codex posted additional review comments mid-session while coverage was still running, requiring re-prioritization and a second patch cycle.
Full cargo xtask ci coverage is slow (spans wasm adapter targets and browser suites), creating a long feedback loop between patch and confirmation.
open_items: Codex review threads need to be individually replied to with commit references and resolved — this was deferred until after the push.
Only focused tests were verified before the second commit; full coverage rerun was in progress at session end.
